task_bundle_catalog.name: Operation catalog, schemas, and normalized result
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Land and validate the frozen 18-operation registry, closed input schemas, exact nine-field AgentOperationResult, canonical digests, and contract-case references before adapters move behavior.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Kernel context, scope, policy, ownership, and compatibility
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Create the one query/execute/recover authority; resolve exactly one configured project and re-evaluate risk, ownership, compatibility, and locks immediately before mutation.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Operation journal, lock convergence, and recovery
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Persist prepared before effects, implement state transitions/index/retention, same-ID digest rules, bounded correlation, and evidence-only reconciliation without replay.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Task lifecycle and resource integration
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Route start/stop/restart/run through the Kernel while retaining existing run, process, port, ownership, and state authorities; enforce reuse and verified ownership.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Bounded observations
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Implement query-bound high-water cursors and bounded/redacted capabilities, diagnosis, inspection, status, logs, events, and operation correlation.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Digest-bound safe clean
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Separate clean.plan from clean.applySafe, canonicalize plan digest, journal/lock/recompute before delete, and preserve protected state/evidence.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Read-only adoption inspection
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Implement bounded adoption.inspect over trusted project context with zero writes, zero registration/start, and no adoption.apply authorization.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Backward-compatible CLI adapter
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Incrementally route shared operations through the Kernel while preserving schemaVersion 1 fields, mirrors, compact output, text, statuses, exit codes, and broader CLI-only routes.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Typed local stdio MCP adapter
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Use the pinned SDK transport, project exactly 18 tools, map schema/protocol/domain errors, serialize protocol-only stdout, and keep policy/state in the Kernel.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Canonical MCP-first Skill
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Update the one canonical Skill to capability-probe, observe, invoke one safe operation, recover uncertainty, narrowly fallback before dispatch, and reject unsafe/raw/deferred routes.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Non-destructive Skill installer and diagnosis
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Scan current, legacy, project, and Plugin copies; digest-classify identical/divergent states; install only to the current path and never automatically overwrite/delete divergent user content.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Compatibility manifest and build identity
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Keep version axes independent, compute canonical component digests/build identity, expose capabilities, and fail writes closed on incompatible or tampered components.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Prebuilt runtime and dual Plugin generation
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Build one isolated Node 20 ESM runtime and separately render policy-free Codex and Claude packages with host-correct paths and integrity sidecars.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Readiness evidence and claim gating
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Record exact build/surface/operation/host/version/OS/architecture/scope/Node/scenario evidence cells, invalidate stale cells, and prohibit unsupported rollups.

task_bundle_catalog.name: Deferred and dropped capability guards
task_bundle_catalog.task_strategy: Encode the three user-confirmed deferrals and four explicit exclusions in schemas, capabilities, Skill traces, Plugin generation, docs, and negative tests without removing preserved CLI/local/read/recovery behavior.

obligation_groups.name: One Kernel, normalized semantics, dynamic policy, and CLI compatibility

mp_mapping.gate: No adapter, Skill, or Plugin may own an alternate lifecycle/policy/state authority.
All production, test, packaging, and evidence paths remain rooted in F:/github/launchdeck.
CLI schemaVersion 1 and Agent protocol/version axes remain independent and both validate.

ca_mapping.gate: Every CLI/MCP mutation reaches the same Kernel and ownership gate; direct adapter mutation fails coverage.
MCP annotations remain descriptive and cannot alter authorization.
CLI and Plugin runtime block incompatible writes and expose exact diagnostic axes.
Current configured/effective risk is evaluated at execution, not copied from a tool hint.
Package version alone never authorizes shared-state writes.
Every normalized result contains surface/runtime/state/build provenance without conflating trust with path.
All shared mutation entrypoints enforce the same dynamic task-risk decision.
Host approval and MCP annotations never elevate Kernel permissions.
outcome.kind and resource.status remain independently asserted in every branch.
CLI, Agent, config, registry, journal, and package versions never collapse into one axis.
Adapter comparison normalizes allowed formatting/provenance differences and fails on semantic drift.

obligation_groups.name: Configured project, lifecycle, ownership, reuse, and task lifetime

mp_mapping.gate: First release remains configured-project, low-risk, local stdio, one canonical Skill, and two generated host artifacts.

ca_mapping.gate: Repeated/concurrent matching start produces one spawn/run and reusedExistingRun=true thereafter.
Unknown/external/probable/stale ownership remains inspect-only; PID/port alone never permits mutation.
Every MCP call resolves one configured project and never uses Plugin cwd as project authority.
Public task inputs reject command/cwd/env/force recursively and select declared tasks only.
Plugin installation scope and executable project scope remain distinct; project scope cannot cross roots.
Closing MCP/host leaves the managed task live and observable under the same run/state identity.
adoption inspection never chains registration, configuration write, or task.start.

obligation_groups.name: Journal, uncertainty, recovery, idempotency, and clean digest

mp_mapping.gate: Every uncertainty path stops at evidence-based get/list/reconcile; no automatic replay across surfaces.

ca_mapping.gate: Only digest-bound safe clean is public; risky clean and model-authored yes stay excluded.
Post-dispatch uncertainty cannot cause CLI fallback or a second mutation.
Kernel recomputes under locks and returns plan_digest_mismatch before deletion on any drift.
A plan digest proves identity/freshness only and never stands in for human confirmation.
confirmed/yes/approvalToken and equivalent nested fields are absent and rejected.
Every mutation has a durable prepared record before resource effects and preserves recovery evidence.
Indeterminate task.run remains get/reconcile-only and handler count never increases during recovery.
Product/test language promises resource-aware recovery, not universal exactly-once execution.
Same ID/same digest returns or reconciles the original operation without handler execution.
Indeterminate work is never automatically retried by CLI, MCP, or Skill.

obligation_groups.name: Canonical Skill, installer, compatibility, generated Plugins, and lifecycle takeover

mp_mapping.gate: One canonical intent Skill remains MCP-first and safety-preserving; divergent copies block a single-active claim.
Host/Plugin lifecycle changes component presence only; services and shared state survive for compatible takeover.

ca_mapping.gate: Plugin update/uninstall does not migrate, prune, or delete shared control-plane state.
Disable/update/uninstall preserves registry, journal, logs, events, config, and managed task.
Current/legacy/project/Plugin Skill digests are diagnosed; divergent content is never overwritten/deleted automatically.
npm runtime, Codex artifact, and Claude artifact are distinct outputs sharing one compatibility/build identity.
Shipped runtime launches without global CLI, npx, install scripts, Plugin-local node_modules, or external npm imports.
Node >=20 prerequisite is explicit; old/missing Node withholds the exact cell.
Skill/runtime/schema/manifest/host metadata share one build identity; tamper blocks writes.
Plugin lifecycle cannot signal tasks or delete LAUNCHDECK_HOME state.
Host manifests contain identity/path/presentation only and no policy, operation, state, recovery, or authorization rules.
Compatible build B sees build A runId/PID/port/stateHome and safely manages it without respawn.
After uninstall, standalone CLI observes logs/events/state/run and safely takes over; reinstall does not duplicate state.

obligation_groups.name: Bounded observation, real stdio, Skill traces, host/OS evidence, and claim discipline

mp_mapping.gate: Every release claim names exact evidence cells; missing/failed/blocked/unsupported/stale cells stay visible.

ca_mapping.gate: Logs/events use bounded cursor reads and never arbitrary paths or permanent follow.
Every stdout record is protocol JSON; banners/diagnostics/task output make the cell fail.
CLI evidence cannot fill MCP, Plugin, host, update, uninstall, recovery, or OS cells.
Codex and Claude install/routing/lifecycle cells remain independent until both pass.
Skill validation asserts ordered tool/command traces and forbidden calls, not plausible final prose.
MCP-ready evidence comes from the shipped runtime over real stdio, never handler-only tests.
Each host-ready claim includes a real installation/use trace for the exact host/version/OS/scope/build.
Docs/capabilities/release labels cannot roll up beyond exact current passing evidence cells.

obligation_groups.name: User-confirmed future semantics retained as deferral, not first-release execution

ca_mapping.gate: Public MCP rejects medium risk; no elicitation/execute transition ships.
Future confirmation requirements stay documented only and no confirmation-shaped public inputs appear.
adoption.inspect is delivered, adoption.apply is absent, and inspect output never authorizes a write/start.

capability_coverage.claim_gate: Registry and both adapters demonstrate one Kernel-owned semantic result path.
Missing/ambiguous/conflicting/unregistered/plugin-cwd scope cases all refuse distinctly.
Risk, ownership, project scope, and compatibility are re-evaluated under locks before each mutation.
Crash/response-loss/same-ID/missing-ID fixtures prove recovery without replay.
All migrated shared routes preserve schemaVersion 1 fixtures, compact projection, text, and exit codes.
Shipped stdio runtime lists exactly 18 tools and passes real initialize/list/call/error/purity/restart/concurrency flows.
All observation operations are query-bound, redacted, bounded, cursor-safe, and non-following.
Low-risk lifecycle positive/reuse and medium/external/unknown refusal paths pass through both public adapters.
Known/lost-ID recovery and stale clean-plan refusal show no replay/deletion.
Byte/inventory/process/port snapshots prove bounded adoption.inspect has zero writes and no apply authorization.
Deterministic traces and real-host traces prove MCP-first and narrow pre-dispatch fallback; duplicate migration is non-destructive.
Separate Codex/Claude artifacts share one tested identity but independently pass host path/install/lifecycle cells.
Evidence registry contains exact current cells and refuses unsupported rollup.
Deliver adoption.inspect and negative absence/zero-write guards only; adoption.apply is not implemented.
Public MCP/Skill refuses medium risk; existing CLI behavior is preserved but never used as Agent fallback.
Only local stdio/Node Plugins are built or claimed; remote/UI/native/enterprise surfaces remain absent.
Public registry/tools/capabilities/Skill/Plugin exclude high/destructive/force/remove/risky/reset/raw/follow behavior; existing CLI-only compatibility routes are not deleted.
External/unknown inspection remains available but every termination/mutation path refuses.
No universal exactly-once claim; journaled resource idempotency, explicit uncertainty, and recovery remain delivered.
Local configured-project control remains delivered while remote/production targets and claims are absent.

acceptance_coverage.claim_gate: All shared contract branches compare semantically and CLI envelope remains backward-compatible.
Plugin-started run survives host exit and compatible standalone CLI observes/stops the same run.
Repeated and concurrent cross-surface start records one spawn and reuse semantics.
External/unknown fixtures survive every attempted public mutation and Skill does not force/fallback.
Medium/high/destructive/raw/external/model-confirmation cases refuse despite host hints and never fallback.
Prepared precedes effects; known/lost-ID recovery works and uncertain work never replays.
Same-ID/same-digest recovers; different digest and missing/expired refuse with no new work.
Every drift class yields plan_digest_mismatch before deletion; fresh digest deletes safe targets only.
Every exact host fixture proves explicit project resolution and plugin-cwd non-authority.
Full build A -> lifecycle change -> build B/CLI same-run takeover chain passes per exact host/OS cell.
Capabilities reports actual independent axes/digests/host/scope/evidence and tamper/incompatibility blocks writes.
Tool traces prove MCP-first, narrow pre-dispatch safe fallback, and refusal/uncertainty no-fallback.
Current/legacy scans classify identical/divergent copies and no automatic overwrite/delete occurs.
Shipped runtime passes the complete real stdio suite on each exact claimed OS cell.
Separate real Codex and Claude install/routing/lifecycle/takeover cells pass on every platform claimed ready.
Before/after snapshots prove adoption.inspect zero writes and every surface excludes adoption.apply authorization.

real_entrypoint_paths.entrypoint_gate: Spawn the real CLI process or packaged bin; direct handler tests alone do not cover this chain.
Use the shipped bundle and a real MCP SDK client; no direct server-handler substitute.

chain: one safe operation or typed refusal
fallback_chain: MCP unavailable before dispatch or safe operation explicitly unsupported
real_entrypoint_paths.entrypoint_gate: Any business refusal, sent/possibly-sent mutation, raw/force/risky/remote intent, or ambiguous recovery forbids fallback.

chain: optional explicit user action and post-action rescan
real_entrypoint_paths.entrypoint_gate: Automatic install/update never overwrites or deletes divergent user content; Plugin copy is reported separately.

chain: node plus artifact-relative runtime and plugin-root cwd candidate
real_entrypoint_paths.entrypoint_gate: Claim-gated by exact real Codex version/OS/architecture/scope/Node/build cell; relative cwd/path behavior is not considered proven by source inspection.
Claim-gated by exact real Claude version/OS/architecture/scope/Node/build cell including spaces/non-ASCII path and mid-session identity behavior.

real_entrypoint_paths.surface: Plugin update, disable, uninstall, and takeover
chain: Plugin build A starts one managed run
prove task and shared state survive
compatible Plugin build B and standalone CLI observe same run
real_entrypoint_paths.entrypoint_gate: Install, update, disable, uninstall, and takeover are distinct evidence dimensions; the full chain is required for lifecycle readiness.

chain: response channel lost at controlled fault point
known ID -> operation.get/reconcile OR lost ID -> one bounded operation.list
real_entrypoint_paths.entrypoint_gate: Recovery must traverse public CLI or real MCP routes and the durable journal; test-only direct record mutation is insufficient.

chain: exact receipt persisted and rollup recalculated
real_entrypoint_paths.entrypoint_gate: One OS/host cell never substitutes for another; current Windows planning workspace is not readiness evidence.

validation_tasks.name: Registry, schema, result, and exclusion contract suite
assertions: closed recursive schemas and forbidden-input rejection
all operation definitions have handler/schema/policy/contract refs
exact nine-field result with outcome/resource/effects invariants
deferred/dropped/CLI-only IDs absent from public projections
validation_tasks.evidence: Machine-readable schema/catalog validation and per-operation case matrix.

validation_tasks.name: Real CLI compatibility and CLI/MCP semantic equivalence
assertions: existing CLI schemaVersion 1 fixtures/compact/text/exit codes remain
allowed surface/runtime path/generated ID differences only
validation_tasks.evidence: Raw CLI JSON/exit code, real MCP structured result, normalized diff, and state/effect receipts.

validation_tasks.name: Project scope, dynamic risk, ownership, and compatibility refusal matrix
validation_tasks.evidence: Typed results, no-effect snapshots, external fixture survival, and adapter equivalence receipts.

validation_tasks.name: Task lifecycle reuse, concurrency, and ownership suite
validation_tasks.evidence: Append-only spawn/signal receipts, run/state records, port observations, and normalized results.

validation_tasks.name: Journal crash, retention, correlation, and no-replay suite
assertions: fault before prepared has no effect
validation_tasks.evidence: Durable records/revisions/index receipts, independent handler/effect counters, and public recovery transcripts.

validation_tasks.name: Safe-clean digest and protected-state suite
validation_tasks.evidence: Before/after tree digests, plan payload/digest, journal transitions, and refusal/positive results.

validation_tasks.name: adoption.inspect bounded zero-write suite
validation_tasks.evidence: CLI/MCP outputs plus before/after state/process/port receipts and exclusion scan.

validation_tasks.name: Bounded observation cursor/redaction suite
assertions: no arbitrary paths or permanent follow
malformed record warning without mutation authority
validation_tasks.evidence: Cursor fixtures, page transcripts, redaction assertions, and no-effect receipts.

validation_tasks.name: Real MCP stdio protocol suite
validation_tasks.evidence: SDK-client transcript, byte-level stdout/stderr capture, runtime/build identity, and journal/state receipts.

validation_tasks.name: Canonical Skill trace and forbidden-call evaluation
validation_tasks.kind: deterministic eval plus real host trace
assertions: pre-dispatch unavailable/unsupported safe CLI fallback only
validation_tasks.evidence: Machine-readable tool/command traces and host discovery/routing receipts.

validation_tasks.name: Skill installation and migration matrix
assertions: current and legacy Codex plus project/Plugin copies
validation_tasks.evidence: Before/after trees, content-manifest digests, installer/doctor results, and user-content preservation receipts.

validation_tasks.name: Compatibility, build identity, and tamper suite
validation_tasks.evidence: Golden canonical payloads, manifest validation, tamper matrix, capabilities and fail-closed results.

validation_tasks.name: Deterministic Plugin generation and isolated bundle suite
validation_tasks.kind: automated build plus native OS execution
validation_tasks.evidence: Build/metafile/archive/integrity receipts and BUNDLE-* cells for each OS.

validation_tasks.name: Host-agnostic Plugin lifetime and compatibility chain
validation_tasks.kind: automated state/process contract plus host precondition
assertions: compatible build B same run identity
validation_tasks.evidence: One chained run/state/ownership/build timeline; does not replace VT-015 or VT-016 host evidence.

validation_tasks.name: Real Codex install, routing, lifecycle, and takeover
validation_tasks.evidence: CODEX-INSTALL-* and CODEX-LIFECYCLE-* cells; status starts not_executed_host_owned.

validation_tasks.name: Real Claude Code install, routing, lifecycle, reload, and takeover
validation_tasks.evidence: CLAUDE-INSTALL-* and CLAUDE-LIFECYCLE-* cells; status starts not_executed_host_owned.

validation_tasks.name: Exact Windows/macOS/Linux evidence matrix
validation_tasks.claim_gate: Every placeholder is replaced with recorded exact values at execution; every cell begins not executed and proves only itself.

validation_tasks.name: Evidence registry invalidation and readiness-rollup refusal
assertions: capabilities/docs expose exact evidence refs only
validation_tasks.evidence: Rollup fixtures with intentional missing/stale cells and no broad ready boolean.

validation_tasks.name: Deferred/dropped capability exclusion guards
assertions: adoption.apply and medium-risk Agent execution absent

validation_tasks.name: Repository baseline and published-entrypoint regression
assertions: existing smoke/package checks exposed by live package scripts
validation_tasks.evidence: Command receipts, package inventory, CLI fixture diff, and repository-root provenance.

user_confirmed_deferrals.exact_excluded_behavior: First-release adoption.apply and every automatic project/user configuration write, registry mutation, or implicit task start.
user_confirmed_deferrals.residual_risk: Users receive inspection and a proposed plan only; adoption remains manual until a separately reviewed digest/confirmation contract exists.
user_confirmed_deferrals.reopen_or_stop_condition: Stop and reopen specification if adoption writes or apply become a required delivery outcome.
user_confirmed_deferrals.downstream_artifact: Generated tasks implement adoption.inspect plus zero-write/absence guards only; tasks.md must not contain adoption.apply implementation.
user_confirmed_deferrals.does_not_remove: CAP-010 adoption.inspect and manual existing adoption/configuration workflows outside the public Agent apply surface.

user_confirmed_deferrals.exact_excluded_behavior: First-release public MCP and Skill-fallback execution for medium-risk tasks.
user_confirmed_deferrals.residual_risk: Medium-risk configured tasks remain available only through existing standalone CLI policy and are not an Agent fallback target.
user_confirmed_deferrals.reopen_or_stop_condition: Stop and reopen when a target host proves a portable human-elicitation contract and medium-risk Agent execution is requested.
user_confirmed_deferrals.downstream_artifact: Generated tasks prove low-risk-only MCP/Skill refusal and preserve future digest-bound confirmation semantics as documentation only.
user_confirmed_deferrals.does_not_remove: Existing CLI medium-risk behavior or the future prepare/confirm/revalidate design record.

user_confirmed_deferrals.exact_excluded_behavior: Remote MCP/OAuth, hooks, MCP UI, GUI/TUI, native executable packaging, enterprise managed policy, and production/remote service control.
user_confirmed_deferrals.residual_risk: First-release use requires local Node 20+ and stdio host integration; no remote or visual management surface exists.
user_confirmed_deferrals.reopen_or_stop_condition: Stop and create a new specification if any deferred surface becomes a required delivery outcome or readiness claim.
user_confirmed_deferrals.downstream_artifact: Generated tasks and release evidence contain no implementation or readiness rollup for these surfaces.
user_confirmed_deferrals.does_not_remove: Local CLI, local stdio MCP, canonical Skill, Node-based Codex/Claude Plugins, or local configured-project lifecycle management.

dropped_exclusion_guards.excluded_behavior: High/destructive public MCP, force-stop, project removal, risky clean, reset, arbitrary shell/env/cwd/force, model confirmation fields, and permanent follow.
dropped_exclusion_guards.does_not_remove: Existing backward-compatible CLI-only force/risky/project-management/follow routes; they remain outside tools/list, capabilities.agentOperations, Skill fallback, and Plugin manifests.
dropped_exclusion_guards.guard: VT-001/009/010/019 must prove recursive input and operation absence plus preserved CLI-only fixtures.
dropped_exclusion_guards.stop_and_reopen_condition: A new reviewed safety contract explicitly adds any excluded public Agent capability.

dropped_exclusion_guards.excluded_behavior: External-process termination or mutation based on port/PID alone.
dropped_exclusion_guards.does_not_remove: project.inspect, task.status, conflict diagnosis, and bounded evidence for external/unknown processes.
dropped_exclusion_guards.guard: VT-003/004/019 must prove inspectability and survival under every mutation attempt.
dropped_exclusion_guards.stop_and_reopen_condition: Ownership semantics are explicitly redefined in a new discussion/specification.

dropped_exclusion_guards.does_not_remove: Operation IDs, prepared-before-effects journal, same-ID recovery, resource-aware idempotency, explicit indeterminate outcomes, and get/list/reconcile.
dropped_exclusion_guards.guard: VT-005/018/019 must prove no replay while retaining explicit possible/unknown effects and no exactly-once marketing language.
dropped_exclusion_guards.stop_and_reopen_condition: A separately bounded operation class proves stronger semantics without changing the general promise.

dropped_exclusion_guards.does_not_remove: Local configured-project lifecycle operations and local Codex/Claude Plugin use.
dropped_exclusion_guards.guard: VT-003/009/010/019 must prove no remote target/auth/transport/production operation or Skill route exists.
dropped_exclusion_guards.stop_and_reopen_condition: Remote/production control is requested under a new trust, authentication, policy, and evidence contract.

coverage_gaps.gap: No operation journal, operation list/get/reconcile routes, deterministic fault points, or readiness evidence registry exists in current live code.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: VT-005, VT-014, VT-017, and VT-018 remain required tasks; recovery/lifecycle/readiness cannot be marked complete from planning artifacts.

coverage_gaps.gap: The MCP SDK, Zod, esbuild, node-semver, stdio server, bundle, compatibility manifest, and generated Plugin artifacts are not present in current package evidence.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: VT-009, VT-012, and VT-013 must execute against the shipped artifact before any MCP/bundle claim.

coverage_gaps.gap: Current CLI is the orchestration composition root; shared routes have not been cut over to the Kernel or proven semantically equivalent.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: VT-002 and VT-020 must preserve schemaVersion 1 and existing CLI-only routes.

coverage_gaps.gap: The canonical Skill remains CLI-reference based and the installer still permits forced divergent overwrite and legacy Codex user targeting.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: VT-010/011 must prove MCP-first routing and non-destructive current/legacy/plugin handling.

coverage_gaps.gap: No real Codex or Claude installation/routing/update/disable/uninstall/takeover receipt exists on any OS.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: All VT-015/016/017 host cells start not executed; SU-002 remains open for each exact cell.

coverage_gaps.gap: Exact supported Windows/macOS/Linux versions, architectures, host versions, and installation scopes are not pinned.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: Replace VT-017 placeholders with exact support dimensions before execution and never broaden a passed cell.

coverage_gaps.gap: Current synchronous and asynchronous lifecycle locks differ, and stable observation cursors/canonical input-digest fixtures do not exist.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: Lock authority, cursor, and digest contract tests must pass before shared mutation cutover.

coverage_gaps.gap: Project cognition graph is missing/stale; plan ownership claims rely on bounded live repository evidence.
coverage_gaps.claim_gate: Task compilation and implementation must recheck touched live owners; reopen if evidence contradicts the one-authority map.

stop_and_reopen: Any CLI, MCP, Skill, or Plugin path requires a second lifecycle backend, state namespace, policy authority, or Plugin-owned task lifetime.
CLI schemaVersion 1 public field/status/compact/exit-code compatibility cannot be preserved while mapping Kernel results.
A first-release public Agent requirement adds medium/high/destructive execution, arbitrary shell/env/cwd/force, risky clean, external termination, permanent follow, or remote/production control.
adoption.apply, configuration writes, registry mutation, or implicit task start becomes first-release scope.
A host cannot launch the isolated prebuilt Node 20 runtime without global Launchdeck, npx, install script, Plugin-local node_modules, or external runtime assets.
Journal/lock design cannot durably persist prepared before effects or cannot prevent replay of indeterminate work.
A real Codex/Claude host contract contradicts the generated path/manifest strategy and cannot be fixed within policy-free host templates.
Live repository evidence contradicts existing registry/run/ownership/state authority or requires moving implementation outside F:/github/launchdeck.
Any requested release statement exceeds exact current evidence cells.

integration_targets.need: Compile TB-001 through TB-015 into dependency-ordered implementation and validation tasks; preserve complete first-release scope, all five-field deferrals, all four exclusion guards, and every claim gate.

Index every generated task against exact CA/MP/CAP/AC refs, validation task refs, real-entrypoint paths, phase owner, and deferred/dropped disposition without treating evidence tasks as implementation completion.
Own TB-001..TB-007 and VT-001/003/004/005/006/007/008 before adapter cutover; one Kernel/state authority is a hard dependency.

integration_targets.target: CLI and MCP adapter task group
integration_targets.need: Own TB-008/TB-009 and VT-002/009 using RP-CLI and RP-MCP real entrypoints, preserving CLI compatibility and real stdio evidence separately.
Own TB-010/TB-011 and VT-010/011, including real ordered traces, current/legacy/plugin digest matrix, and narrow fallback boundary.

integration_targets.target: compatibility, build, and Plugin task group
integration_targets.need: Own TB-012/TB-013 and VT-012/013; generated Codex/Claude contents may pass generation before real-host readiness but must never be labeled host-ready.
Own TB-014 and VT-014..VT-018; create exact host/OS/build cells, retain not-executed/blocked/unsupported states, and gate rollups.
Own TB-015 and VT-019, ensuring CAP-014..016 five-field records and CAP-017..020 does-not-remove boundaries are visible in generated tasks and acceptance review.
Reject completion if any CA/MP/CAP/AC becomes unmapped, if any real-entrypoint chain is replaced solely by handler/mock evidence, or if a readiness claim lacks exact evidence-cell receipts.
